content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 8, 1989

Herman Leavitt
Treasurer, TIP
Educational Fund
1219 28th Street, NW
Washington, DC 20007

Re: FPPC No. I-89-47

Dear Mr. Leavitt:

This is in response to your request for information regarding
the 1989 legislative changes to the Political Reform Act.

Enclosed for your information are the following items:

Political Reform Act as amended to January 1, 1989
Proposition 68 with cover memo
Proposition 73
Proposition 73 Information Sheet
Formal Opinion to Charles H. Bell, Jr., FPPC No. 88-002
Commission regulations 18502E, 18502.1, 18502.2E, 18536,
18536.1 & 18536.2
FPPC Bulletins for December 1988 & January 1989

Your name has been added to the mailing list for the monthly
FPPC Bulletin. Also, I will send the "Interim Manual on
Proposition 68 and Proposition 73," the regular campaign
information manual and the 1989 campaign reporting forms as soon
as they are available.

If you have any questions please call me at (916) 322-5662 or
write the Commission at the address below.

Sincerely,

Kim Marie Taylor
Staff Services Analyst

Enclosures

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

TO INSURE PROGRESS

tip POLITICAL ACTION FOR THE:

HOTEL EMPLOYEES AND RESTAURANT
EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION

1219 28TH STREET, N.W. • WASHINGTON, D.C. 20007 • (202) 393-4373

January 18, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir/Madam:

I am Treasurer of a union political action committee based in Washington, D.C.
and registered with California campaign finance authorities. It is my understanding
from a brief news item in the Washington Post that California voters recently
approved new restrictions on campaign contributions. I understand also that there may
be some confusion in determining the regulations to implement these restrictions because
there are two separate and in some cases conflicting initiatives involved. While I
understand final rules may not have been promulgated, I would appreciate receiving any
information you may have such as copies of the initiatives themselves, fact sheets, draft
regulations whatever. Sketchy information is better than none which is what I presently
would.

It is also my understanding based on conversations with your staff last year, that
you routinely update your information manual each year but that copies are not
automatically sent to filing committees unless requested. Please consider this letter
such a request. I am particularly concerned with receiving a list of 1989 reporting dates.

Thank you for your assistance.

Cordially,

Herman Leavitt
Treasurer, TIP
Educational Fund

HL/kod

TO INSURE PROGRESS

POLITICAL ACTION FOR THE

1219 28TH STREET, N.W. • WASHINGTON, D.C. 20007 • (202) 393-4373

January 18, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir/Madam:

I am Treasurer of a union political action committee based in Washington, D.C. and registered with California campaign finance authorities. It is my understanding from a brief news item in the Washington Post that California voters recently approved new restrictions on campaign contributions. I understand also that there may be some confusion in determining the regulations to implement these restrictions because there are two separate and in some cases conflicting initiatives involved. While I understand final rules may not have been promulgated, I would appreciate receiving any information you may have such as copies of the initiatives themselves, fact sheets, draft regulations whatever. Sketchy information is better than none which is what I presently have.

It is also my understanding based on conversations with your staff last year, that you routinely update your information manual each year but that copies are not automatically sent to filing committees unless requested. Please consider this letter such a request. I am particularly concerned with receiving a list of 1989 reporting dates.

Thank you for your assistance.

Cordially,

Herman Leavitt
Treasurer, TIP
Educational Fund

HL/kod

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 25, 1989

Herman Leavitt
Treasurer, TIP
Educational Fund
1219 - 28th St., N.W.
Washington, D.C. 20007

Re: Letter No. 89-047

Dear Mr. Leavitt:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on January 23, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: A Washington D.C.-based union political action committee treasurer requests information about California's newly approved campaign contribution restrictions (Propositions 68 and 73) and updated campaign finance reporting materials and dates.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC acknowledges receipt of the advice request and provides the requested materials including copies of the initiatives, regulations, bulletins, and adds the requester to the mailing list for future updates, with additional materials to follow when available.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of three pieces: incoming request (January 18), acknowledgment letter (January 25), and substantive response with enclosures (February 8). This is technically an informal advice response as classified, though it primarily furnishes requested reference materials rather than formal legal guidance. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are present; the question and conclusion are synthesized from the request and response content.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A Washington D.C.-based union political action committee treasurer requests information about California's newly approved campaign contribution restrictions (Propositions 68 and 73) and updated campaign finance reporting materials and dates.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges receipt of the advice request and provides the requested materials including copies of the initiatives, regulations, bulletins, and adds the requester to the mailing list for future updates, with additional materials to follow when available.

embedding.summary: This document package contains an informal advice request from a D.C.-based union PAC treasurer seeking clarification on California's new campaign finance restrictions and reporting requirements, followed by two FPPC responses providing the requested informational materials and acknowledging the request with timeline expectations.
